articles.title: Josh Hokit Hijacked the UFC Press Conference
articles.description: Chael Sonnen says this was one of the greatest UFC press conferences of all time — and it had almost nothing to do with the scheduled fights. What started as a promotional event for the White House card quickly turned into complete chaos when Josh Hokit decided he wasn’t interested in building a fight with Derrick Lewis. Instead, he targeted Alex Pereira nonstop. Pereira tried to stay disciplined and avoid engaging. According to Chael, that was probably the correct strategy. But Hokit kept pressing, escalating the situation until Ilia Topuria finally snapped after feeling his own spotlight disappear. Things escalated fast: Pereira refused to respond. Hokit kept attacking verbally. Topuria got involved. Something got thrown. Security stepped in. Hokit was removed from the stage And somehow… the man who got kicked out may have still won the press conference. Chael breaks down the psychology, performance, and strategy behind one of the strangest UFC media events in recent memory — and why press conferences in MMA are more like performance art than sport. #UFC #IliaTopuria #AlexPereira #JoshHokit #MMA #ChaelSonnen #DerrickLewis Timestamps 0:00 Don’t Watch the Press Conference 0:38 UFC and “Martial Arts as Art” 1:42 A Top 3 Press Conference Ever 2:20 This Was Supposed to Be Topuria’s Day 3:05 Josh Hokit Changes the Plan 4:00 Why Hokit Targeted Pereira 5:05 Pereira Refuses to Engage 6:12 Hokit Keeps Pressing 7:08 Topuria Finally Snaps 7:52 Something Gets Thrown 8:20 Security Removes Hokit 8:52 Did Hokit Actually Win? articles.title: The Simple Reason Chimaev Dominates #ufc
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev isn’t beating people with flashy techniques—he’s beating them with fundamentals done at an elite level. In this clip, the conversation breaks down the simplicity behind Chimaev’s dominance. At the center of it all? The double-leg takedown. No spinning attacks. No complicated setups. Just relentless pressure, timing, and execution. The strategy to stop it sounds simple too: sprawl, take your legs away, and get back to your feet immediately. That’s the key. Wrestlers become less dangerous when opponents refuse to stay controlled on the bottom. But here’s the problem—knowing what’s coming and stopping it are two very different things. For years, critics claimed Chimaev’s style would fail over 25 minutes because of fatigue. Yet fight after fight, he keeps proving that narrative wrong. Even when tired, the pressure stays the same. The pace doesn’t collapse. And that consistency is exactly why he’s become one of the most feared champions in MMA. Simple doesn’t mean easy. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, wrestling analysis, and real insight into the fight game. #KhamzatChimaev #UFC #MMA #Wrestling #DoubleLeg #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: The Worst Conor Fight Idea
articles.description: Conor McGregor vs Max Holloway is being talked about. But according to Chael Sonnen, it’s the one fight you absolutely can’t do. In this breakdown, Chael explains why he doesn’t believe the fight will happen—and why it shouldn’t. This isn’t about popularity. Both guys are stars. This is about something much simpler. You can’t sell something the audience has already seen. Chael breaks down why Conor’s return is too valuable to waste on a rematch, especially when there are so many new matchups available. Because if Conor only fights one more time, it has to matter. It has to feel new. It has to move the story forward. And going backward is the one thing you can’t afford to do. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that shouldn’t be made. #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Max’s Team Reaches Out0:40 Why Chael Doesn’t Believe It1:20 The Real Problem2:10 You Can’t Do This Fight3:00 Why Conor’s Return Matters4:00 The “Only Rule”5:00 Better Options6:00 Final Take Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: “He Can Ragdoll Him!” DJ Predicts Chimaev vs Strickland
Chimaev’s Biggest Threat Isn’t Striking #ufc
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev might be one of the most dominant wrestlers in MMA—but there’s a style that could give him real problems. In this clip, the focus shifts to a key detail many fans overlook: getting takedowns is one thing… keeping elite American wrestlers down is another. Fighters like Bo Nickal come from a system built around exploding off the bottom, refusing to stay controlled, and constantly forcing scrambles. That’s a completely different challenge. Chimaev’s wrestling pressure is undeniable, especially with his double-leg entries and relentless chain attacks. But American wrestling emphasizes not only takedown defense—it rewards getting back to your feet immediately. That skillset changes the entire dynamic of control in MMA. And the comparison gets even more interesting when experience enters the conversation. Chimaev has a lifetime of wrestling experience, but not necessarily a lifetime of riding and controlling elite wrestlers who won’t accept bottom position. Fighters with deep MMA experience and scrambling ability could create situations Chimaev hasn’t consistently faced. That’s the real question: who can stop the pressure without breaking? Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, wrestling analysis, and real fight game insight. #KhamzatChimaev #BoNickal #UFC #MMA #Wrestling #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: What If Gina Carano Misses Weight?
articles.description: What if Gina Carano misses weight? Because according to Chael Sonnen, that might be the most important part of the entire fight. In this breakdown, Chael explains why the build to Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano feels incomplete—and why the usual details don’t seem to matter. The rounds. The weight class. Even the reason for the fight. None of it has become the story. But one thing could. Gina Carano is expected to make 145 pounds, which would be the lowest weight of her career. After years away. After openly talking about getting back into shape. And now scheduled for five rounds on top of it. Chael breaks down why that combination raises real questions, and why the outcome of the weigh-in could determine everything that follows. Because if she misses, the fight changes. And if she makes it, that might be the most impressive moment of the entire event. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that don’t make sense—until they do. #UFC #MMA #GinaCarano #RondaRousey #ChaelSonnen 0:00 The Real Question 0:40 Missing Fight Details 1:20 Why This Is Unusual 2:00 The 145 Pound Issue 3:00 Gina’s Weight Cut 4:00 Why 5 Rounds Matters 5:00 What If She Misses?6:00 If She Makes It 7:00 Final Take Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Roy Jones Jr. Could Ruin Gaethje #ufc
articles.description: Roy Jones Jr. helping Justin Gaethje sounds legendary—but it could also be a disaster. In this clip, the concern isn’t about Roy’s greatness. It’s about applying boxing strategy to MMA against a dangerous puncher like Ilia Topuria. Roy’s advice focuses on defense—slipping punches, rolling with shots, taking the sting off incoming strikes. In boxing, that works. In MMA with 4-ounce gloves? That’s a completely different story. The margin for error in MMA is razor thin. You can’t stand in the pocket planning to absorb shots and expect to survive. One clean connection changes everything. Defensive reactions that are safe in boxing become high-risk decisions in the cage. That’s what makes this matchup so dangerous. If Gaethje enters the fight expecting to manage Topuria’s power instead of overwhelming him, the entire dynamic shifts the wrong way. This is one of the biggest differences between boxing and MMA—and it’s why crossover advice isn’t always transferable. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #JustinGaethje #IliaTopuria #RoyJonesJr #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: Conor vs Max? Hard Pass #ufc
articles.description: Max Holloway knocking out Conor McGregor in a commercial instantly sparked rumors—but is this fight actually happening? In this clip, the growing buzz around McGregor vs Holloway gets broken down, and there’s one major issue: why are we revisiting a fight that already happened? Fans have waited years for Conor’s return, and if he finally comes back, shouldn’t it feel fresh? The matchup raises even bigger questions when you look at the weight. Taking two natural 155-pound fighters and putting them at 170 creates a strange situation—especially for Holloway, who has no real future at welterweight. Meanwhile, there are multiple compelling options for McGregor at both 155 and 170 that would feel bigger, newer, and more meaningful. Nobody doubts the star power. Nobody doubts the attention it would get. But that doesn’t automatically make it the right fight. This is the difference between a fight fans will watch… and a fight fans actually asked for. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #UFCReturn #CombatSports

articles.title: Strickland vs Chimaev: Who’s Telling the Truth?
articles.description: Sean Strickland vs Khamzat Chimaev isn’t just about the fight. It’s about what happened before it. In this breakdown, Chael Sonnen explains why he’s less interested in the actual fight—and more interested in the sparring sessions that led up to it. Because the stories don’t match. At one point, Strickland seemed to give Chimaev all the credit. Later, he walked that back. Now there’s footage. But only a piece of it. And that raises a bigger question. What really happened in those rounds? Chael breaks down why gym dynamics, media narratives, and selective clips can all shape perception—and why none of it tells the full story. Because when fighters remember things differently, and no one else steps forward, the truth gets harder to find. So where’s the rest of the footage? And who’s going to tell the full story? Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights before the fight. #UFC #MMA #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #ChaelSonnen 0:00 The Real Story Isn’t the Fight0:40 Why Sparring Matters1:20 Strickland’s Early Comments2:20 Why Fighters Say That3:20 The Story Changes4:20 The Missing Footage5:20 Where’s the Third Party?6:20 Why This Matters7:00 Final Take Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Tom Aspinall Will NOT Be the Same
articles.description: Tom Aspinall will not be the same. And according to Chael Sonnen, that might change everything. In this breakdown, Chael explains why the version of Aspinall fans remember may already be gone. This isn’t about ability. It’s about mindset. Chael breaks down the shift from competitor to prizefighter, and why that transition rarely works the way people expect. Injuries, frustration, and time away from the cage have all played a role. But the bigger concern is what comes next. Because the next fight won’t be easier. Whether it’s Ciryl Gane again or a new opponent like Alex Pereira, the challenge only gets harder from here. And if the belt were taken away tomorrow, would Aspinall be able to fight his way back to it? That’s the real question. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fighters who have to reinvent themselves. #UFC #MMA #TomAspinall #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Tom Aspinall Isn’t the Same0:40 What Changed?1:30 Competitor vs Prizefighter2:30 Why That Matters3:20 The Next Fight Gets Harder4:20 Gane vs Pereira5:20 If He Loses the Belt6:20 Can He Get It Back? articles.title: “You Didn’t Know Me!” DJ And Joshua Van On Being MMA Fans
The “Gas Tank” Myth Is Wrong for Chimaev #ufc
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev gets labeled as a fighter who “gasses out.” But where’s the proof? In this clip, that narrative gets challenged head-on. Yes, Chimaev uses a ton of energy early—but being tired isn’t the same as being ineffective. The real question is simple: when has he actually faded? Even in high-pressure fights, including against Kamaru Usman, Chimaev kept pushing forward. No drop-off. No mental break. No shift in approach. That’s the difference. Fatigue only matters if it changes performance—and so far, there’s little evidence that it does. Meanwhile, Sean Strickland is known for something different: consistency over long fights. Four rounds, five rounds—steady pressure, steady pace. So now you’ve got two narratives colliding. One says Chimaev slows down. The other says he doesn’t—and until proven otherwise, that claim holds up. This isn’t about who gets tired. Everyone gets tired. It’s about who changes when they do. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #KhamzatChimaev #SeanStrickland #KamaruUsman #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #MMANews #CombatSports

articles.title: “He Can Go Far!” DJ Breaks Down Bedtime MMA Debut
Who Deserves Islam? It’s Complicated #ufc
articles.description: Carlos Prates might be one of the best fighters at 170 pounds—but that doesn’t mean he’s next in line. In this clip, the welterweight picture gets messy fast. Prates looks elite, especially when you compare performances against Jack Della Maddalena and how that stacks up with Islam Makhachev. On paper, you could argue Prates looked even more dangerous. But then comes the setback—Ian Machado Garry complicates everything. Now the debate opens up. Does Garry get the shot? Does Prates still deserve it? Or does a rising name like Michael Morales jump the line? And here’s where it gets even more interesting—there are two prizes at 170 right now. One is the title fight. The other? Conor McGregor. And depending on how things shake out, those opportunities might go to completely different fighters. That leaves Prates in a strange position. He’s winning, he’s dangerous, and he’s relevant—but timing and matchmaking might work against him. This is what chaos looks like in a stacked division. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #CarlosPrates #UFC #MMA #IslamMakhachev #IanGarry #MichaelMorales #ConorMcGregor #FightTalk

articles.title: The Truth About Pereira vs Gane
articles.description: Alexander Volkov spoke up.But according to Chael Sonnen, he didn’t help himself. In this breakdown, Chael reacts to Volkov’s comments about Alex Pereira vs Ciryl Gane not being a “real” championship fight.And while the statement might be technically true, it misses the point.Because if you’re going to step into the conversation, you need to make it count.Volkov had an opportunity.A chance to remind everyone he already beat Gane.A chance to argue for his own place in the title picture.A chance to move himself forward.Instead, Chael believes he did the opposite.By downplaying the fight, Volkov may have taken himself out of the conversation entirely.So what was the goal?Because in this sport, every interview is an opportunity..And if you don’t use it, someone else will.Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the moments fighters can’t afford to waste. #UFC #MMA #AlexanderVolkov #AlexPereira #CirylGane #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Volkov Speaks Out0:30 Not a “Real” Title Fight?1:10 Why This Was Strange1:50 The Gane Fight Revisited2:30 Missed Opportunity3:10 What Volkov Should’ve Said3:50 How This Hurts Him4:30 Final Take Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: JOSHUA VAN on CONTROVERSIAL PANTOJA WIN, NOT KNOWING WHO DJ IS?! | EXCLUSIVE INTERVIEW!!
articles.description: THE CHAMP IS HERE! "Mighty" Demetrious Johnson welcomes the UFC flyweight champ Joshua Van before his defends his title vs Tatsuro Taira at UFC 328 on the latest edition of "The MightyCast"! articles.title: Conor vs Holloway Again? No One Wants This
articles.description: Conor McGregor vs Max Holloway is being talked about again.And according to Chael Sonnen, that’s a problem. In this breakdown, Chael reacts to a video game trailer that shows Holloway knocking out Conor and what that could actually mean..Is it just promotion?.Or is it hinting at a real fight?Because if it is, Chael isn’t convinced it’s the right move.When it comes to Conor’s return, there’s only one thing fans really want..Something new.Not a fight we’ve already seen..Chael breaks down why this matchup doesn’t check the right boxes, especially at 170 pounds where neither fighter truly belongs long term..There are other opponents.Other options.Fights that actually move things forward.And with limited opportunities left to see Conor compete, every one of them matters.So why go backward?Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the fights that shouldn’t be happening. #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Video Game Trailer Reaction0:40 Is This a Real Fight Hint?1:20 Why Fans Miss Conor2:00 The One Thing Fans Want2:40 We’ve Already Seen This Fight3:20 The 170 Problem4:00 Why This Doesn’t Work4:40 Better Options for Conor5:10 Final TakeBad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Sean Strickland Has A Secret Weapon For Khamzat!?
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev vs Sean Strickland is finally here, and after all the drama, threats, beef, and talk about whether this fight would even make it to the Octagon, we’re now looking at one of the most interesting middleweight title fights possible. Michael Bisping breaks down why Khamzat is such a terrifying force, from his explosive wrestling and ridiculous shot speed to the strength Robert Whittaker felt first-hand. But Sean Strickland brings a very different kind of challenge: relentless pressure, constant sparring, awkward striking, toughness, and the ability to drag opponents into a gruelling fight. Can Strickland survive the early storm, stop the takedowns, and force Khamzat into deep waters? Or is Chimaev about to prove that nobody at middleweight is taking that belt from him any time soon? Subscribe for more UFC 328 coverage, fight breakdowns, predictions, and daily MMA content. 🔒 Become a Member for special Perks, Exclusive Content and ultra-exclusive Live Chats: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCDrG2_1TcVkXKXXsD6Kjwig/join 🤖 Join The Discord: https://discord.gg/fbb9pc59 ▶️ Follow Michael on Social: https://www.instagram.com/mikebisping https://www.twitter.com/bisping https://www.facebook.com/mikethecountbisping ▶️ Edited by John Brannigan: https://www.instagram.com/johnbranniganuk https://www.twitter.com/johnbranniganuk #bisping #ufc #mma #mixedmartialarts #michaelbisping #ufcnews #mmanews #ufc328 #khamzat #khamzatchimaev #seanstrickland

articles.title: Tom Aspinall’s Update Raises Bigger Questions
articles.description: Tom Aspinall has finally spoken. And according to Chael Sonnen, his update raises more questions than answers. In this breakdown, Chael reacts to Aspinall’s comments about his eye injury, including the claim that he’s progressing well but still not cleared to spar. That alone is interesting. Because along the way, there have been multiple versions of this story. From the UFC saying the injury wasn’t serious, to talk of surgery, to now hearing that he’s still working toward clearance. So what’s actually true? Chael breaks down the mixed messaging, including comments from Aspinall’s manager and what it could mean for his next fight. With plans to face the winner of Alex Pereira vs Ciryl Gane, the timeline suddenly matters more than ever. And there’s one moment that could tell you everything. If Aspinall shows up in person for that fight, it signals confidence. If he doesn’t, it may say the opposite. Because right now, it’s not about what’s being said. It’s about what happens next. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the questions no one is answering. #UFC #MMA #TomAspinall #ChaelSonnenBad' Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: “Force Him To Strike!” DJ Breaks Down Khamzat vs DDP
This Grudge Isn’t What You Think...
articles.description: Dominick Cruz says the face-off between Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev should be canceled. According to Chael Sonnen, that might be exactly why fans want to see it even more. In this breakdown, Chael reacts to the growing narrative around this fight being one of the most intense grudges in MMA. But when you actually look at the history, the story doesn’t quite match the hype. These two fighters trained together. They were once in the same room, working under the same system, without any major issues. The idea that this is some long-standing personal rivalry doesn’t line up with how things actually unfolded. So where did it come from? Chael breaks down how narratives build over time, how fighters can talk themselves into believing a rivalry, and how the promotion side of the sport can amplify something that wasn’t originally there. That doesn’t mean the fight isn’t real. It just means the story might not be. Because when you strip everything back, this isn’t about hate. It’s about competition. Watch more breakdowns and get the real story behind the rivalries that aren’t what they seem. #UFC #MMA #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Cruz Wants It Canceled 0:40 Now You Want It More 1:30 Is This Really a Grudge? 2:30 The Gym History 3:30 Where This Started 4:30 The Fight That Didn’t Happen 5:30 How Narratives Build 6:30 Is This Real or Not? 7:30 The Face-Off Is Happening Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench
